searchDocs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
searchDocs.content: Frequently Asked Questions This section contains answers to frequently asked questions about QA Assistants, a WordPress plugin. Coming Soon FAQ content is currently under development. Please check back later for complete information.

searchDocs.title: Welcome to QA Advisor Documentation
searchDocs.content: Welcome to the new documentation site for QA Advisor WordPress Analytics! This blog will contain updates about new features, tutorials, and best practices for using QA Advisor. Stay tuned for more content coming soon.

searchDocs.title: QA Advisor β (v4.9.0-beta) - June 5, 2025​
searchDocs.content: WordCamp Europe 2025 Special Beta Release featuring revolutionary heatmap analytics for WordPress.

searchDocs.title: Release History​
searchDocs.content: Browse through our release history organized by year:  2025 - Current year releases v4.9.0-beta - QA Advisor Beta (June 5, 2025)

searchDocs.title: Version Numbering​
searchDocs.content: We follow semantic versioning:  Major (X.0.0): Breaking changes or major feature additionsMinor (4.X.0): New features and improvementsPatch (4.9.X): Bug fixes and minor improvementsBeta/RC (4.9.0-beta): Pre-release versions

searchDocs.title: Update Notifications​
searchDocs.content: Stay updated with the latest releases:  📧 Subscribe to our newsletter🐦 Follow us on Twitter📱 Enable update notifications in your WordPress admin panel

searchDocs.title: Assistants
searchDocs.content: Assistants Task-focused helpers that suggest next actions from your site data. How to install/use: coming soonBrowse Assistants ↗︎ (official site): https://quarka.org/en-assistants/

searchDocs.title: 🎉 Overview​
searchDocs.content: We're excited to announce the beta release of QA Advisor (formerly QA Analytics) at WordCamp Europe 2025! This beta version represents a major evolution in WordPress analytics, introducing powerful new features for understanding user behavior through visual analytics.

searchDocs.title: 🔥 Heatmap Analytics​
searchDocs.content: Click Heatmaps: Visualize where users click most frequently on your pagesScroll Depth Tracking: Understand how far users scroll down your contentMovement Heatmaps: Track mouse movement patterns to identify user attention areasMobile & Desktop Views: Separate heatmap data for different device types

searchDocs.title: 📊 Enhanced Analytics Dashboard​
searchDocs.content: Real-time Data Visualization: See user interactions as they happenAdvanced Filtering: Filter data by date range, device type, and user segmentsExport Capabilities: Download heatmap data and reports for further analysis

searchDocs.title: 🚀 Performance Improvements​
searchDocs.content: Optimized Data Collection: Minimal impact on site performanceEfficient Storage: Smart data compression for heatmap informationFaster Loading Times: 30% improvement in analytics dashboard loading

searchDocs.title: 🔧 Developer Features​
searchDocs.content: New API Endpoints: Access heatmap data programmaticallyCustom Event Tracking: Define your own interaction eventsIntegration Hooks: Easy integration with other WordPress plugins

searchDocs.title: 📋 System Requirements​
searchDocs.content: WordPress 5.0 or higherPHP 7.4 or higherMySQL 5.7 or higherModern browsers (Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+)

searchDocs.title: ⚠️ Beta Notice​
searchDocs.content: This is a beta release intended for testing and feedback. While we've extensively tested QA Advisor, you may encounter some issues:  Some features may change before the final releaseData migration from beta to stable version will be supportedPlease report any bugs or feedback through our GitHub repository

searchDocs.title: 🔜 Coming in Summer 2025​
searchDocs.content: The stable release of QA Advisor is planned for Summer 2025 and will include:  Enhanced heatmap accuracyAdditional visualization optionsAdvanced user journey trackingAI-powered insightsMulti-site supportAnd much more!

searchDocs.title: 📥 Download​
searchDocs.content: Download the beta version from our official website or directly from the WordCamp Europe 2025 booth.

searchDocs.title: 🙏 Acknowledgments​
searchDocs.content: Special thanks to the WordPress community and all beta testers who are helping us make QA Advisor the best analytics solution for WordPress.    Thank you for trying QA Advisor Beta! We look forward to your feedback.

searchDocs.title: How display modes work​
searchDocs.content: Display modes control which menus appear, not a toggle inside each report.  Simple (default) shows: Assistants / Realtime / Audience / Settings / HelpAdvanced adds: Acquisition / Landing Pages / Top Growing / All Pages / Goals  To enable Advanced Mode: Settings → Advanced Mode.

searchDocs.title: Menu at a glance (visibility / Heatmap & Session Replay)​
searchDocs.content: Simple / Advanced indicate whether the menu is visible in that mode.  Menu	Simple	Advanced	What it’s for	Heatmap	Session ReplayAssistants	✔	✔	Task-focused assistants (e.g., Page Analysis, Social, Overview)	–	– Realtime	✔	✔	Real-time visits and behavior	–	✔ Audience	✔	✔	Site-wide audience and sessions	–	✔ Acquisition ✔	Sources and campaign effectiveness	–	– Landing Pages ✔	Entry pages performance	✔	– Top Growing ✔	Pages with notable growth	✔	– All Pages ✔	Compare performance across all pages	✔	– Goals ✔	Conversions and goal completion	✔	✔ Settings	✔	✔	Configuration (incl. mode switch)	–	– Help	✔	✔	Help & Support (Docs / Support Forum / Debug Info)	–	–

searchDocs.title: Assistants at a glance​
searchDocs.content: Assistants are add-on, task-focused helpers you can enable from the “Assistants” menu. They provide context-aware suggestions and next actions based on your site data.  📌 What are Assistants?🧩 Browse Assistants ↗︎ (official site)

searchDocs.title: Installation​
searchDocs.content: Download and install QA Assistants on your WordPress site in just a few minutes.

searchDocs.title: Environment Setup​
searchDocs.content: Make sure your server, plugins, and caching settings are optimized for accurate tracking.

searchDocs.title: What You'll Learn​
searchDocs.content: ✅ How to install QA Assistants on WordPress✅ How to configure your environment for reliable tracking✅ System requirements and compatibility✅ Basic configuration and settings✅ Privacy and GDPR compliance setup✅ Troubleshooting common issues✅ Best practices for analytics tracking

searchDocs.title: Time Required​
searchDocs.content: Installation: 5 minutesEnvironment Setup: 5–10 minutesFirst Data: Within 1 hour

searchDocs.title: Before You Begin​
searchDocs.content: Make sure you have:  Administrator access to your WordPress siteWordPress 5.9 or higherPHP 7.0 or higherA modern web browser    Ready to begin? Start with the Installation Guide →

searchDocs.title: Change Monthly PV Limit and Data Retention
1) File to edit​
searchDocs.pageTitle: Change Monthly PV Limit and Data Retention
searchDocs.content: Path: wp-content/qa-zero-data/qa-config.phpThis file already exists with default values.The copy inside the plugin folder (wp-content/plugins/qa-heatmap-analytics/qa-config.php) is a template and is not loaded.  Before you edit Edit only the two settings on this page (Monthly PV limit / Data retention). Leave other lines as they are. You may add your own PHP comments (e.g., // note)?that’s safe.  Show other constants you might see (do not edit) QAHM_CONFIG_USE_LSCMD_LISTFILE QAHM_CONFIG_TWO_SYSTEM_MODE QAHM_CONFIG_SYSTEM_MODE QAHM_CONFIG_CPROC_NUM_MAX QAHM_CONFIG_RCNK_MAX QAHM_CONFIG_SOCIAL_REFERRER QAHM_CONFIG_BEHAVIORAL_SEND_INTERVAL QAHM_CONFIG_HTML_DIFF_DETECTION_MODE (Names may vary by version.)

searchDocs.title: 2) How to edit​
searchDocs.pageTitle: Change Monthly PV Limit and Data Retention
searchDocs.content: Open wp-content/qa-zero-data/qa-config.php using one of the following:  Hosting file manager (edit in place), orFTP/SFTP/SSH (download → edit → upload back), orA server-side editor (e.g., SSH + vim/nano)

searchDocs.title: A) Change the monthly PV limit​
searchDocs.pageTitle: Change Monthly PV Limit and Data Retention
searchDocs.content: Find this line (or add it if missing):  define( 'QAHM_CONFIG_LIMIT_PV_MONTH', 10000 ); // Monthly PV limit (integer, >= 1)   Replace 10000 with your limit (integer, 1 or greater). Examples:  // Small site define( 'QAHM_CONFIG_LIMIT_PV_MONTH', 10000 ); // Staging / test define( 'QAHM_CONFIG_LIMIT_PV_MONTH', 2000 ); // High-traffic (adjust to server capacity) define( 'QAHM_CONFIG_LIMIT_PV_MONTH', 50000 );   Save (or upload back) the file.

searchDocs.title: B) Change the data retention (days)​
searchDocs.pageTitle: Change Monthly PV Limit and Data Retention
searchDocs.content: Find this line (or add it if missing):  define( 'QAHM_CONFIG_DATA_RETENTION_DAYS', 120 ); // Data retention in days (integer, >= 1)   Replace 120 with how many days to keep data (integer, 1 or greater). Examples:  // Production define( 'QAHM_CONFIG_DATA_RETENTION_DAYS', 120 ); // Staging / test define( 'QAHM_CONFIG_DATA_RETENTION_DAYS', 14 ); // Larger window define( 'QAHM_CONFIG_DATA_RETENTION_DAYS', 180 );   Save (or upload back) the file.  Input rules for both settings: use integers ? 1. 0 and negative values are invalid.

searchDocs.title: 3) How to confirm​
searchDocs.pageTitle: Change Monthly PV Limit and Data Retention
searchDocs.content: In WordPress, go to QA Assistants → Settings → General Settings.Scroll to Data retention & limits. Monthly PV Limit and Data retention shown there should match the numbers set in wp-content/qa-zero-data/qa-config.php. Reports will use these values: Monthly PV Limit applies to new data immediately.Data retention removes older data automatically once per night. The maintenance timing is fixed and not user-configurable.

searchDocs.title: Troubleshooting​
searchDocs.pageTitle: Change Monthly PV Limit and Data Retention
searchDocs.content: I edited a file but nothing changed → Confirm you edited wp-content/qa-zero-data/qa-config.php (not the plugin template). Data didn’t shrink right away → Old data is cleaned up once per night by an automatic job. The schedule is fixed and cannot be changed from the plugin. My number was rejected → Use integers only, and 1 or greater.

searchDocs.title: Notes (optional)​
searchDocs.pageTitle: Change Monthly PV Limit and Data Retention
searchDocs.content: Keep a quick backup before edits (e.g., qa-config.php.bak).Do not commit wp-content/qa-zero-data/qa-config.php to your repository.

searchDocs.title: What Can Go Wrong​
searchDocs.content: If jquery.js is loaded with the defer attribute, it may delay jQuery's availability, causing the QA Assistants script to run before jQuery is ready. This can lead to:  Tracking data not being recorded ("no data")Heatmaps failing to display, even though page data existsJavaScript errors that may interfere with other plugins

searchDocs.title: How to Avoid This​
searchDocs.content: To ensure proper functionality, please consider one of the following approaches:  Do not apply defer to jQuery. This is the most reliable way to preserve the correct script order. Include QA Assistants’ tracking script as part of your defer queue, but after jQuery. For example, if you are managing defer behavior in functions.php, be sure to include QA Assistants' scripts (qa-heatmap-analytics/js) after jQuery in the defer list.  💡 Based on internal testing, deferring jQuery can prevent the QA Assistants tracking script from initializing correctly.

searchDocs.title: Summary​
searchDocs.content: Always ensure that jQuery is available before the QA Assistants tracking script runs. This prevents loss of data and ensures your heatmaps and analytics work as expected.

searchDocs.title: 1. JavaScript Compression & Minification​
searchDocs.content: Do not compress, minify, or combine JavaScript files used by QA Assistants. Some optimization plugins or themes may interfere with tracking scripts by modifying or delaying their execution.  ✅ Check your caching or optimization plugin settings ✅ Disable JS minification or defer/async for QA Assistants scripts  For more technical background, see When jQuery is Deferred.

searchDocs.title: 2. PHP Memory Limit & Execution Time​
searchDocs.content: QA Assistants processes tracking data during daily aggregation, which can be memory-intensive on larger sites.  We recommend the following PHP settings:  memory_limit: 1024M or highermax_execution_time: 240 seconds or more  💡 These settings can usually be adjusted via php.ini, .htaccess, or your hosting control panel. If you're unsure, ask your hosting provider.

searchDocs.title: 3. Plugin & Theme Compatibility​
searchDocs.content: QA Assistants works with most major plugins and themes. However, we recommend:  Disabling JavaScript defer/async for critical scriptsEnsuring your caching plugin allows output from qa-heatmap-analyticsAvoiding duplication with other tracking tools that modify global events

searchDocs.title: 4. CDN & Server Migration​
searchDocs.content: If you're using a CDN (e.g., Cloudflare) or recently migrated your server:  Clear all caches (browser, plugin, CDN)Confirm that QA Assistants scripts are not blocked or delayedMake sure your server time zone is correct (used for data grouping)

searchDocs.title: 5. Optional: Confirm Script Output​
searchDocs.content: To verify that the tracking script is being correctly loaded:  Visit your website while not logged inRight-click and choose “View Page Source”Search the HTML for qahm or qahmz  If these strings are not found, the script may be blocked or not loading. Check for JavaScript errors or plugin conflicts.

searchDocs.title: Prerequisites​
searchDocs.content: Before installing QA Assistants, ensure your system meets the following requirements:  WordPress: Version 5.9 or higherPHP: Version 7.0 or higherMySQL: Version 5.7 or higherBrowser: Modern browser (Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+)

searchDocs.title: Step 1: Download the Plugin​
searchDocs.content: Download the latest qa-heatmap-analytics.zip file from the top page of this documentation site. Go to Documentation Top

searchDocs.title: Step 2: Install via WordPress Admin (Recommended)​
searchDocs.content: Log in to your WordPress admin dashboardNavigate to Plugins → Add NewClick the Upload Plugin buttonSelect the downloaded qa-heatmap-analytics.zip fileClick Install NowOnce installed, click Activate Plugin

searchDocs.title: (Optional) Install via FTP​
searchDocs.content: Extract the qa-heatmap-analytics.zip file on your computerUse an FTP client to upload the extracted folder to /wp-content/plugins/Activate the plugin from the WordPress admin → Plugins

searchDocs.title: (Optional) Install via WP-CLI​
searchDocs.content: If you have WP-CLI installed:  # Install from local file wp plugin install qa-heatmap-analytics.zip --activate

searchDocs.title: Verify Installation​
searchDocs.content: Check that QA Assistants is working:  Visit your site's frontendReturn to WordPress adminGo to QA Assistants → RealtimeYou should see initial tracking data

searchDocs.title: Enable Advanced Mode (Optional)​
searchDocs.content: QA Assistants offers an Advanced Mode that unlocks additional features such as Audience insights, Acquisition reports, and Goal tracking. To enable it:  Go to QA Assistants → SettingsCheck the Advanced Mode optionClick Save Changes

searchDocs.title: (Optional) Customize Monthly Data Collection Limit​
searchDocs.content: By default, QA Assistants collects up to 10,000 pageviews per month. You can change this limit by adding a line to your qa-config.php file. Learn how to configure the limit →

searchDocs.title: Plugin Won’t Activate?​
searchDocs.content: If the plugin fails to activate, please check the following:  Make sure PHP version is 7.0 or higherEnsure WordPress is up to dateDisable other plugins to test for conflicts

searchDocs.title: No Data Showing?​
searchDocs.content: If no data appears after installation, please consider the following:  Tracking data in the Dashboard (available in Advanced Mode) is updated only once a day, typically overnight.To check if tracking is working immediately, go to QA Assistants → Realtime in the admin menu.Logged-in WordPress users are not tracked by default.Clear your browser cache and wait 5–10 minutes after activation to allow the first data to appear.If no activity is shown even in the Realtime view after visiting your site, the tracking script might not be loading correctly.

searchDocs.title: Performance Issues​
searchDocs.content: If your site slows down:  Check server resourcesOptimize database tables

searchDocs.title: Menu Overview
searchDocs.content: Menu Overview Quick reference for all QA Assistants menu screens. Simple and Advanced columns show which display mode includes each menu. To switch modes: Settings → Advanced Mode. Menu	Simple	Advanced	What it shows	Heatmap	Session Replay	DocsRealtime	✔	✔	Live visits and recent activity	–	✔	Realtime Audience	✔	✔	Site-wide audience and sessions	–	✔	Reports Acquisition ✔	Traffic sources and campaigns	–	–	Reports Landing Pages ✔	Entry page performance	✔	–	Reports Top Growing ✔	Rising content discovery	✔	–	Reports All Pages ✔	All pages comparison	✔	–	Reports Goals ✔	Conversions and goal tracking	✔	✔	Goals Settings	✔	✔	Configuration and mode switch	–	–	Settings Help	✔	✔	Docs, support, and debug info	–	–	Help tip If a menu item isn’t visible, enable Advanced Mode in Settings.

It will include: Creating your first heatmapUnderstanding heatmap types (click, scroll, movement)Filtering and segmenting dataExporting heatmap reportsBest practices for heatmap analysis

searchDocs.title: Overview​
searchDocs.content: The Help screen provides comprehensive support resources, documentation links, troubleshooting guides, and contact information for QA Assistants plugin assistance. This section serves as your first stop for resolving issues, learning about features, and accessing professional support when needed.

searchDocs.title: Accessing This Screen​
searchDocs.content: Log in to your WordPress admin panelNavigate to the left sidebar menuClick on "QA Assistants"Select "Help" from the submenu

searchDocs.title: Screen Layout​
searchDocs.content: The Help screen includes:  Quick Start Guide: Essential setup and configuration stepsDocumentation Library: Links to comprehensive user guides and tutorialsTroubleshooting Section: Common issues and their solutionsSupport Contact Options: Ways to reach technical supportSystem Information: Technical details about your installationCommunity Resources: Forums, knowledge base, and user community links

searchDocs.title: Documentation Access​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Provide easy access to comprehensive documentation and tutorialsHow to use: Browse documentation categories or search for specific topicsOptions: User manual, developer documentation, API references, and video tutorialsTips: Bookmark frequently referenced documentation sections for quick access

searchDocs.title: Troubleshooting Tools​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Help diagnose and resolve common plugin issuesHow to use: Use diagnostic tools and follow troubleshooting guides for specific problemsOptions: Automated diagnostics, manual troubleshooting steps, and system checksTips: Run diagnostics before contacting support to gather relevant information

searchDocs.title: Support Contact Methods​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Provide multiple ways to get help when self-service options aren't sufficientHow to use: Choose the appropriate support channel based on your issue urgency and license typeOptions: Email support, live chat, phone support, and community forumsTips: Include system information and error details when contacting support

searchDocs.content: Access the Help screen and review the Quick Start GuideFollow the initial setup instructions step by stepConfigure basic tracking and privacy settingsTest the installation to ensure proper functionalityExplore additional features using the documentation links

searchDocs.title: Troubleshooting Issues​

searchDocs.content: Gather relevant information about your issue (error messages, system info, steps to reproduce)Check your license type to determine available support optionsChoose the appropriate contact method (email, chat, phone)Provide detailed information about your problemFollow up on support tickets and implement recommended solutions

searchDocs.title: Diagnostic Settings​
searchDocs.content: Enable or disable automatic diagnostic data collectionConfigure system information sharing preferencesSet up automated health checks and monitoring

searchDocs.title: Help Content Not Loading​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Help documentation or resources fail to loadSolution: Check internet connection and firewall settingsCheck: Verify that external content loading is not blocked by security plugins

searchDocs.title: Diagnostic Tools Not Working​
searchDocs.content: Issue: System diagnostic tools fail to run or provide incomplete informationSolution: Check file permissions and PHP configurationCheck: Ensure that diagnostic functions are not disabled by hosting restrictions

searchDocs.title: Support Contact Issues​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Unable to reach support or submit support ticketsSolution: Verify contact information and check spam filtersCheck: Ensure that your license is active and support is included

searchDocs.title: Outdated Help Content​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Help documentation appears outdated or doesn't match current plugin versionSolution: Clear browser cache and check for plugin updatesCheck: Verify that you're viewing documentation for the correct plugin version

searchDocs.title: Search Function Not Working​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Help search functionality returns no results or incorrect informationSolution: Try different search terms or browse categories manuallyCheck: Ensure that search indexing is up to date and functioning properly

searchDocs.content: Settings - Configure plugin settings and preferencesExternal: Community forums

searchDocs.title: Overview​
searchDocs.content: The Goals screen allows you to set up, monitor, and analyze conversion tracking for your website. This feature helps you measure how well your site achieves specific business objectives, such as form submissions, purchases, newsletter signups, or any other valuable user actions you want to track.

searchDocs.title: Accessing This Screen​
searchDocs.content: Log in to your WordPress admin panelNavigate to the left sidebar menuClick on "QA Assistant"Select "Goals" from the submenu

searchDocs.title: Screen Layout​
searchDocs.content: The Goals screen features:  Goal Setup Wizard: Step-by-step interface for creating new goalsActive Goals List: Overview of all configured goals with performance metricsConversion Funnel Visualization: Visual representation of goal completion pathsGoal Performance Charts: Trends and patterns in goal completion over timeAttribution Analysis: Understanding which traffic sources drive conversionsGoal Value Tracking: Revenue or value attribution for completed goals

searchDocs.title: Goal Configuration​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Set up tracking for specific user actions that matter to your businessHow to use: Use the goal setup wizard to define conversion events and conditionsOptions: Track page visits, form submissions, downloads, purchases, or custom eventsTips: Start with your most important business objectives and add more goals gradually

searchDocs.title: Conversion Tracking​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Monitor how many visitors complete your defined goalsHow to use: Review conversion rates and completion numbers for each goalOptions: View conversions by time period, traffic source, or user segmentTips: Set realistic conversion rate benchmarks based on your industry and content type

searchDocs.title: Funnel Analysis​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Understand the path visitors take to complete goalsHow to use: Analyze the conversion funnel to identify drop-off pointsOptions: Set up multi-step funnels with intermediate checkpointsTips: Optimize pages with high drop-off rates to improve overall conversion rates

searchDocs.title: Goal Value Assignment​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Assign monetary or point values to goal completionsHow to use: Set up goal values to calculate return on investment for marketing effortsOptions: Use fixed values, dynamic values, or custom calculation methodsTips: Assign realistic values that reflect the actual business impact of each conversion

searchDocs.title: Setting Up Your First Goal​
searchDocs.content: Access the Goals screen and click "Create New Goal"Choose a goal type (destination, duration, pages per session, or event)Define the specific conditions that constitute a goal completionSet a goal value if applicable and save the configurationTest the goal to ensure it's tracking correctly

searchDocs.title: Analyzing Goal Performance​
searchDocs.content: Review the active goals list to see conversion rates and completion numbersIdentify goals with low conversion rates that need optimizationAnalyze trends over time to understand seasonal patternsCompare goal performance across different traffic sources

searchDocs.title: Optimizing Conversion Funnels​
searchDocs.content: Set up funnel visualization for multi-step conversion processesIdentify steps in the funnel with high abandonment ratesAnalyze user behavior at each funnel stepImplement improvements to reduce drop-offs and increase completions

searchDocs.title: Measuring Marketing ROI​
searchDocs.content: Assign appropriate values to each goal based on business impactTrack goal completions by traffic source and campaignCalculate return on investment for different marketing channelsAdjust marketing spend based on goal completion data

searchDocs.title: Goal Types and Triggers​
searchDocs.content: Configure different types of goals (destination, duration, pages, events)Set up custom event tracking for specific user interactionsDefine goal completion conditions and requirements

searchDocs.title: Funnel Configuration​
searchDocs.content: Set up multi-step conversion funnels with intermediate stepsConfigure funnel visualization and analysis optionsEnable or disable funnel requirement settings

searchDocs.title: Attribution Settings​
searchDocs.content: Choose attribution models for goal completion creditConfigure lookback windows for conversion attributionSet up cross-device and cross-session tracking

searchDocs.title: Value and Revenue Tracking​
searchDocs.content: Set up goal values and revenue trackingConfigure dynamic value calculation methodsEnable e-commerce integration for purchase tracking

searchDocs.title: Goals Not Tracking​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Goal completions are not being recordedSolution: Verify that goal conditions are correctly configured and tracking code is properly installedCheck: Test goal completion manually and check for JavaScript errors

searchDocs.title: Inaccurate Conversion Data​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Goal completion numbers seem too high or too lowSolution: Review goal conditions for accuracy and check for duplicate trackingCheck: Verify that goal triggers are firing only when intended

searchDocs.title: Funnel Data Missing​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Conversion funnel shows incomplete or missing dataSolution: Ensure all funnel steps have proper tracking and sufficient trafficCheck: Verify that funnel step URLs and conditions are correctly configured

searchDocs.title: Attribution Issues​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Goal completions are not properly attributed to traffic sourcesSolution: Check attribution model settings and cross-domain tracking configurationCheck: Verify that UTM parameters and referral data are being preserved

searchDocs.title: Related Documentation​
searchDocs.content: Settings - Configure goal tracking preferences

searchDocs.title: Accessing This Screen​
searchDocs.content: Log in to your WordPress admin panelClick QA Assistants in the left sidebarSelect Realtime  Realtime is available in both Simple mode and Advanced mode.

searchDocs.title: Screen Layout​
searchDocs.content: The Realtime screen consists of two main sections:  Realtime Activity — Current user countsSession Recordings — List of completed sessions with session replay

searchDocs.title: Users in the Last 30 Minutes​
searchDocs.content: Displays the number of currently active sessions on your site.  Sessions that have had any activity within the last 30 minutes are counted. Activity includes not only page views and navigation, but also scrolling, clicking, and other interactions.

searchDocs.title: Active Users in the Last 1 Minute​
searchDocs.content: Of the users in the last 30 minutes, this shows the number who have had any interaction within the last 60 seconds.

searchDocs.title: Auto-refresh​
searchDocs.content: User counts are automatically updated every 10 seconds.

searchDocs.title: Overview​
searchDocs.content: This section displays a list of completed user sessions with their browsing data.  Sessions with no activity for more than 30 minutes are processed as "completed." Only sessions that have dwell data (behavioral records such as scrolling) are added to this list.  The list holds a maximum of 5,000 sessions. As new sessions are added, the oldest ones are removed.

searchDocs.title: Table Columns​
searchDocs.content: Column	DescriptionDevice	Device type (PC / Smartphone / Tablet) Exit Time	Last access time of the session Entry Page	First page of the session (with link) Exit Page	Last page of the session (with link) Referrer	Traffic source ("direct" for direct access) PVs	Number of page views in the session Duration	Total session duration Play	Session replay button  The table supports sorting, filtering, and CSV export. It displays 100 entries per page with pagination.

searchDocs.title: Session Replay​
searchDocs.content: Click the play button (video icon) to replay the user's browsing behavior as a movie-like playback in a new window.  note Sessions without dwell data do not appear in the listSession replay is available for today's sessions. For past sessions, use the replay feature in Visit Report

searchDocs.title: Auto-refresh​
searchDocs.content: The session list is automatically updated every 1 minute.

searchDocs.title: Auto-refresh Summary​
searchDocs.content: Target	Refresh IntervalUser counts (30 min / 1 min)	Every 10 seconds Session Recordings list	Every 1 minute  Updates run only while the browser tab is activeWhen the tab becomes inactive, updates pause and resume immediately when you return to the tab

searchDocs.title: Let's Build Data-Driven Plugins​
searchDocs.content: You can create your own plugins using the website data collected by QA Assistants.  "Show me the top 10 pages visited by mobile users last week"  Even without specialized knowledge, data can answer questions like this — this is the world of data-driven assistants. And you can create plugins that extend this world.

searchDocs.title: Introducing QA Assistants API (Preview)​
searchDocs.content: We're developing a REST API that gives you programmatic access to all your analytics data. Whether you're building custom dashboards, integrating with AI tools, or automating reports, the API will be your gateway.

searchDocs.title: Why This Matters​
searchDocs.content: For Plugin Developers:  Access all QA Assistants dataCreate custom report generation pluginsDistribute on WordPress.org and monetizeGrow as part of the developer ecosystem  For AI Integration Enthusiasts:  Connect Claude or ChatGPT to QA AssistantsSupport integration via MCP (Model Context Protocol)Analyze site data in natural languageLet AI analyze traffic patterns and suggest improvements  For Data Utilization Enthusiasts:  Integrate with BigQuery or Looker StudioCombine QA Assistants data with other sourcesAutomate custom reportsSchedule automated reports via scripts

searchDocs.title: What is QAL (Query Assistant Language)?​
searchDocs.content: At the heart of the API is QAL (Query Assistant Language) — a language that empowers data-driven assistants to freely manipulate data. QAL is not a flexible language like SQL; it's specifically designed for QA's column-oriented structure.  QAL Design Philosophy:  Explicit Paths: Clearly describe the data reference path (Material → View → Result)Deterministic: The same query always returns the same resultMinimal Specification: Minimal syntax that neither AI nor humans can misunderstand

searchDocs.title: QAL Basic Structure​
searchDocs.content: A QAL query consists of four elements:  materials: Specify the source data (materials)time: Explicitly specify the analysis period (relative specifications not allowed)make: Build intermediate views step by step from materialsresult: Specify the final view to return

searchDocs.title: A QAL Example​
searchDocs.content: Here's how you request page view aggregation for blog pages:  { "tracking_id": "your-site-id", "materials": [ { "name": "allpv" } ], "time": { "start": "2025-10-01T00:00:00", "end": "2025-10-31T00:00:00", "tz": "Asia/Tokyo" }, "make": { "blog_pages": { "from": ["allpv"], "filter": { "and": [ { "like": { "col": "allpv.url", "val": "%/blog/%" } }, { "eq": { "col": "allpv.device_type", "val": "smp" } } ] }, "keep": ["allpv.url", "allpv.title"], "calc": { "pageviews": "COUNT(allpv.pv_id)", "sessions": "COUNTUNIQUE(allpv.reader_id)" } } }, "result": { "use": "blog_pages", "sort": [{ "by": "pageviews", "dir": "desc" }], "limit": 10 } }   How This Query Works:  Retrieve data from October 2025 using the allpv materialFilter for data where URL contains "/blog/" and device type is mobile (smp)Aggregate page views and sessions by URLReturn top 10 results sorted by page views in descending order

searchDocs.title: 1. Explicit Time Specification​
searchDocs.content: QAL does not allow relative time specifications (like "yesterday" or "last week"). You must always specify concrete dates and times:  "time": { "start": "2025-10-01T00:00:00", "end": "2025-10-31T00:00:00", "tz": "Asia/Tokyo" }   This ensures that the same results are always obtained regardless of when the query is executed.

searchDocs.title: 2. Explicit Data Paths​
searchDocs.content: In QAL, you build the data flow step by step:  "make": { "step1": { "from": ["allpv"], "keep": ["allpv.url", "allpv.reader_id"], "filter": { "and": [ { "like": { "col": "allpv.url", "val": "%/blog/%" } } ] } }, "step2": { "from": ["step1"], "keep": ["step1.url"], "calc": { "sessions": "COUNTUNIQUE(step1.reader_id)" } } }   This step-by-step construction makes it easier to debug than complex SQL and easier for AI to generate.

searchDocs.title: 3. Limited Aggregation Functions​
searchDocs.content: QAL supports only the following six aggregation functions:  COUNT(column) - Count rowsCOUNTUNIQUE(column) - Count unique valuesSUM(column) - Calculate sumAVERAGE(column) - Calculate averageMIN(column) - Get minimum valueMAX(column) - Get maximum value  This limitation ensures predictable and safe query execution.

searchDocs.title: 4. Fully Qualified Names​
searchDocs.content: Column names must always be specified in the format <material_name>.<column_name> or <view_name>.<column_name>:  "keep": ["allpv.url", "allpv.title"], "calc": { "pageviews": "COUNT(allpv.pv_id)" }

searchDocs.title: 1. Device Traffic Analysis​
searchDocs.content: Compare mobile and desktop traffic:  { "tracking_id": "your-site-id", "materials": [{ "name": "allpv" }], "time": { "start": "2025-10-01T00:00:00", "end": "2025-10-31T00:00:00", "tz": "Asia/Tokyo" }, "make": { "device_stats": { "from": ["allpv"], "keep": ["allpv.device_type"], "calc": { "sessions": "COUNTUNIQUE(allpv.reader_id)", "pageviews": "COUNT(allpv.pv_id)", "avg_time": "AVERAGE(allpv.page_msec)" } } }, "result": { "use": "device_stats" } }

searchDocs.title: 2. Campaign Performance Measurement​
searchDocs.content: Analyze performance by UTM campaign:  { "tracking_id": "your-site-id", "materials": [ { "name": "allpv" } ], "time": { "start": "2025-10-01T00:00:00", "end": "2025-10-31T00:00:00", "tz": "Asia/Tokyo" }, "make": { "campaign_performance": { "from": ["allpv"], "filter": { "and": [ { "like": { "col": "allpv.utm_campaign", "val": "%sale%" } } ] }, "keep": ["allpv.utm_campaign", "allpv.utm_source"], "calc": { "sessions": "COUNTUNIQUE(allpv.reader_id)", "pageviews": "COUNT(allpv.pv_id)", "avg_time": "AVERAGE(allpv.page_msec)" } } }, "result": { "use": "campaign_performance", "sort": [{ "by": "sessions", "dir": "desc" }], "limit": 20 } }   How This Query Works:  Retrieve data from October 2025 using the allpv materialFilter for traffic where UTM campaign contains "sale"Aggregate sessions, page views, and average dwell time by campaign and sourceReturn top 20 results sorted by sessions in descending order

searchDocs.title: 3. AI-Powered Insights​
searchDocs.content: By connecting QA Assistants to Claude via MCP (Model Context Protocol), you can analyze data in natural language:  You: Show me the blog posts that are getting the most organic traffic this month Claude: [Generates and executes QAL query] The top 5 posts by organic traffic are...   The AI assistant generates QAL and explains the results in natural language. This allows anyone to interact with data.

searchDocs.title: Available Materials (Data Sources)​
searchDocs.content: Main materials accessible in QAL:

searchDocs.title: 📊 Integrated Page View Log (allpv)​
searchDocs.content: The core unified data source of QA Assistants. This integrated material contains all website behavioral data including page views, sessions, UTM campaigns, device information, and more:  Basic Information:  URL, title, referrerPage dwell time, scroll depthTimestamps  Visitor Information:  Reader ID, session IDDevice type, browser, OSCountry, region information  Marketing Information:  UTM parameters (utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign)Campaign IDTraffic source channel  Engagement Metrics:  Page views, sessionsReading depth, confusion scores (coming soon)Conversion events

searchDocs.title: 🔍 Search Console Data (gsc)​
searchDocs.content: Search performance data:  Keywords and queriesClicks, impressions, CTRSearch appearanceRankings over time  Design Philosophy: QA Assistants' design philosophy is to consolidate data into one unified material (allpv), eliminating the need for complex JOIN operations and providing an intuitive, easy-to-understand data structure for developers. More user behavior metrics will continue to be added to allpv over time.

searchDocs.title: Plugins You Can Build​
searchDocs.content: Using QAL, you can create various data-driven plugins:

searchDocs.title: 📈 Report Automation Plugins​
searchDocs.content: Auto-generate and email weekly reportsCustom KPI dashboardsWhite-label reports for clients

searchDocs.title: 🤖 AI Assistant Plugins​
searchDocs.content: Integrate with ChatGPT or ClaudeAsk data questions in natural languageAuto-generate insights and improvement suggestions

searchDocs.title: 📊 Visualization Plugins​
searchDocs.content: Real-time dashboardsCustom charts and graphsInteractive data exploration tools

searchDocs.title: 🔔 Alert Plugins​
searchDocs.content: Traffic spike/drop notificationsConversion anomaly detectionSEO ranking change alerts

searchDocs.title: 🎯 Personalization Plugins​
searchDocs.content: Content recommendations based on user behaviorA/B test managementDynamic content delivery

searchDocs.title: QA Assistants' Approach​
searchDocs.content: QA Assistants offers a slightly different perspective on utilizing website data.  Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and other excellent tools excel at detailed data analysis and strategic decision-making. QA Assistants, on the other hand, focuses on daily website operations, aiming to understand data and suggest specific next actions.  For example, for everyday content updates or social media posting decisions, QA Assistants' concrete action suggestions might be helpful. At the same time, when developing business strategy, you might perform deeper analysis with GA4's exploration reports and custom dimensions — this kind of role division is one approach.  QA Assistants can be used in combination with existing tools like GA4. We hope you'll consider it as one option for supporting your website's growth while leveraging each tool's strengths.

searchDocs.title: Response Formats​
searchDocs.content: The QAL API offers flexible data return formats:

searchDocs.title: JSON Format (Inline)​
searchDocs.content: For small datasets (up to 50,000 rows):  { "data": [ { "url": "/blog/article-1", "pageviews": 1250, "sessions": 890 }, { "url": "/blog/article-2", "pageviews": 980, "sessions": 720 } ], "meta": { "truncated": false, "row_count": 2 } }

searchDocs.title: File Format (CSV/Parquet)​
searchDocs.content: For large datasets, a file URL is returned:  { "data_url": "https://api.qa-assistant.com/results/abc123.csv", "meta": { "truncated": false, "row_count": 125000, "format": "csv" } }   Specify format in the result section:  { "result": { "use": "my_view", "return": { "mode": "FILE", "format": "CSV" } } }

searchDocs.title: When Can I Use This?​
searchDocs.content: The API is currently in active development. We're refining the design, testing with early users, and preparing comprehensive documentation.  Current Progress:  ✅ Core API design completed✅ QAL specification finalized🚧 Developer documentation in progress🚧 Client libraries and examples being created🚧 MCP server for AI integration under development  Want early access?  If you're interested in being an early tester or have specific use cases in mind, please let us know. Join us on GitHub Issues or QA Assistants Meetup.

searchDocs.title: Stay Updated​
searchDocs.content: This developer manual will be regularly updated as development progresses. Bookmark this page and check back for:  QAL specification updates and best practicesCode examples and tutorialsEarly access announcementsIntegration guides and MCP server information  Questions or ideas? Share them on GitHub Issues or QA Assistants Meetup. Your feedback helps shape the future of data-driven assistance.

searchDocs.title: Common controls​
searchDocs.content: The following controls are shared across report screens.

searchDocs.title: Date range picker​
searchDocs.content: Select a preset period — Last 7 days, Last 30 days, This month, or Last month — or pick a custom range from the calendar.  note Report data is processed overnight. Today's data is not included in these reports. Use Realtime for live activity.

searchDocs.title: Table filters​
searchDocs.content: Click the filter area above a table to add conditions. Available filter types depend on the column:  Text columns — contains, does not contain, equals, not equals, starts with, ends with, empty, not emptyNumeric / percentage / time columns — equals, greater than, less than, empty, not empty

searchDocs.title: Export​
searchDocs.content: Each table has CSV Export and JSON Export buttons. The exported data reflects any active filters.

searchDocs.title: Goal filter​
searchDocs.content: When goals are configured, a radio button group appears to filter by a specific goal. The default is "All Goals."

searchDocs.title: Heatmap links​
searchDocs.content: In the Landing Pages, Top Growing, and All Pages tables, each row shows heatmap icons for Desktop, Tablet, and Smartphone. Click an icon to jump directly to the heatmap view for that page.

searchDocs.title: Audience​
searchDocs.content: The primary report screen, available in both Simple and Advanced modes.

searchDocs.title: Summary chart​
searchDocs.content: A line chart showing Users, Sessions, and Pageviews over the selected period, with totals displayed below.

searchDocs.title: Users / Devices​
searchDocs.content: A breakdown table by user type (New / Returning) and device category (Desktop / Tablet / Smartphone). Columns include sessions, bounce rate, pages per session, avg. session duration, and goal metrics.

searchDocs.title: Session Recording​
searchDocs.content: A list of sessions in the selected period. Click the play button on any row to replay the visitor's browsing activity. Use the goal filter to narrow results to sessions that completed a specific goal.

searchDocs.title: Advanced Export​
searchDocs.content: At the bottom of the page, you can download raw pageview-level data in TSV format for external analysis tools.

searchDocs.title: Acquisition​
searchDocs.content: Advanced Mode only.  Analyze where your traffic comes from, split into two sections:  Channels — Sessions grouped by traffic channel (organic, direct, referral, social, etc.).Source / Medium — Sessions grouped by source × medium combination (e.g. google / organic).  Both sections include a line chart. Check the boxes next to table rows to plot selected channels or sources on the chart.

searchDocs.title: Landing Pages​
searchDocs.content: A table of entry pages — the first page visitors see when they arrive at your site. Key columns: title, URL, pageviews, unique pageviews, avg. time on page, entrances, exit rate, and goal metrics.

searchDocs.title: Top Growing​
searchDocs.content: Identifies landing pages with the highest growth rate. The selected period is split in half, and session counts are compared between the earlier and later halves. Sorted by growth rate (descending) by default.

searchDocs.title: All Pages​
searchDocs.content: A pageview summary of every page on your site — not limited to entry pages. The column structure is similar to Landing Pages.

searchDocs.title: Overview​
searchDocs.content: The Settings screen is the central configuration hub for the QA Assistants plugin, allowing you to customize tracking behavior, privacy settings, data collection preferences, and integration options. This section provides comprehensive control over how the plugin operates on your website and what data it collects.

searchDocs.title: Accessing This Screen​
searchDocs.content: Log in to your WordPress admin panelNavigate to the left sidebar menuClick on "QA Assistants"Select "Settings" from the submenu

searchDocs.title: Screen Layout​
searchDocs.content: The Settings screen is organized into several configuration sections:  General Settings: Basic plugin configuration and site informationTracking Settings: Data collection and analytics preferencesPrivacy Settings: GDPR compliance and user privacy controlsIntegration Settings: Third-party service connections and API configurationsAdvanced Settings: Technical configuration options for experienced usersData Management: Import, export, and data retention settings

searchDocs.title: General Configuration​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Configure basic plugin settings and site identificationHow to use: Set your site name, timezone, and basic tracking preferencesOptions: Site identification, default date ranges, and display preferencesTips: Ensure timezone settings match your business location for accurate reporting

searchDocs.title: Tracking Configuration​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Control what data is collected and how it's processedHow to use: Enable or disable specific tracking features based on your needsOptions: Page views, user interactions, conversion tracking, and custom eventsTips: Balance comprehensive tracking with site performance and privacy requirements

searchDocs.title: Privacy and Compliance​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Ensure compliance with privacy regulations like GDPR and CCPAHow to use: Configure consent management, data anonymization, and user rightsOptions: Cookie consent, IP anonymization, data retention periods, and user opt-outTips: Regularly review privacy settings to maintain compliance with evolving regulations

searchDocs.title: Third-party Integrations​
searchDocs.content: Purpose: Connect QA Assistants with other tools and servicesHow to use: Configure API connections, data sharing, and external service integrationsOptions: Google Analytics, social media platforms, email marketing tools, and CRM systemsTips: Only enable integrations you actively use to minimize data sharing and complexity

searchDocs.title: Initial Plugin Setup​
searchDocs.content: Access the Settings screen after plugin installationConfigure basic site information and timezone settingsSet up tracking preferences based on your analytics needsConfigure privacy settings to comply with applicable regulationsTest tracking functionality to ensure proper operation

searchDocs.title: Updating Privacy Settings​
searchDocs.content: Review current privacy and compliance settingsUpdate consent management and data anonymization optionsConfigure data retention periods according to your privacy policySet up user opt-out mechanisms and data deletion proceduresDocument changes for compliance records

searchDocs.title: Configuring Integrations​
searchDocs.content: Navigate to the Integration Settings sectionSelect the third-party services you want to connectEnter API keys and authentication credentialsConfigure data sharing preferences and sync settingsTest integrations to ensure proper data flow

searchDocs.title: Performance Optimization​
searchDocs.content: Review tracking settings for performance impactDisable unnecessary tracking features to improve site speedConfigure data sampling for high-traffic sitesOptimize database settings and data retention policiesMonitor site performance after configuration changes

searchDocs.title: Tracking Options​
searchDocs.content: Enable or disable specific tracking features (page views, events, conversions)Configure tracking code placement and loading behaviorSet up custom event tracking and goal definitionsAdjust data collection frequency and sampling rates

searchDocs.title: Privacy Controls​
searchDocs.content: Configure cookie consent management and banner displaySet up IP address anonymization and data pseudonymizationDefine data retention periods and automatic deletion schedulesEnable user data access and deletion request handling

searchDocs.title: Performance Settings​
searchDocs.content: Optimize tracking code loading and executionConfigure database query optimization and cachingSet up data archiving and storage managementAdjust real-time processing and batch processing settings

searchDocs.title: Integration Management​
searchDocs.content: Configure API connections and authentication methodsSet up data synchronization schedules and preferencesManage webhook endpoints and data push notificationsControl data sharing permissions and access levels

searchDocs.title: Tracking Not Working​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Analytics data is not being collected or appears incompleteSolution: Verify tracking code installation and check for JavaScript errorsCheck: Ensure tracking settings are properly configured and not blocked by ad blockers

searchDocs.title: Privacy Compliance Issues​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Plugin may not be compliant with privacy regulationsSolution: Review and update privacy settings, enable necessary consent mechanismsCheck: Verify that data collection practices match your privacy policy

searchDocs.title: Integration Failures​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Third-party integrations are not working or syncing dataSolution: Check API credentials, connection settings, and service statusCheck: Verify that external services are operational and API limits are not exceeded

searchDocs.title: Performance Problems​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Plugin is slowing down website loading or causing performance issuesSolution: Optimize tracking settings, reduce data collection frequency, or enable cachingCheck: Monitor server resources and database performance after configuration changes

searchDocs.title: Configuration Not Saving​
searchDocs.content: Issue: Settings changes are not being saved or are reverting to previous valuesSolution: Check file permissions, database connectivity, and browser cacheCheck: Verify that WordPress user has sufficient permissions to modify plugin settings

searchDocs.title: Related Documentation​
searchDocs.content: Goals - Configure conversion tracking settingsHelp - Get support for configuration issues
